title: Goods & Services Tax (GST) Advisory & Filing
shortDescription: End-to-end GST statutory compliance, GSTR-1/3B filings, annual GSTR-9/9C reconciliations, e-invoicing integration, and representation.
fullDescription: Under the Goods and Services Tax framework in India, business operations require meticulous transactional accuracy and timely reporting. Anil Puneet and Associates provides complete GST advisory, compliance, and audit representation services. We assist clients in navigating complex input tax credit (ITC) rules, GST portal reconciliations (GSTR-2B vs. Books), e-invoicing compliance under Rule 48(4), e-way bill management, and handling departmental audit notices under Sections 61, 65, and 73/74.
scopeOfWork: GST Registration and core/non-core amendment filings across multi-state setups
Monthly and quarterly tax filings including GSTR-1, GSTR-3B, and CMP-08
Input Tax Credit (ITC) reconciliation with GSTR-2B to prevent credit leaks and over-claims
Annual Return (GSTR-9) and Reconciliation Statement (GSTR-9C) compilation
E-invoicing implementation, API workflow verification, and IRN management
GST refund application filing for exporters (LUT & inverted duty structure)
Advisory on classification of goods/services, HSN/SAC codes, and GST rates
Drafting replies to GST show cause notices (SCN) and assistance in departmental audits

process.title: Sales & Purchase Data Extraction
process.description: Extracting monthly sales registers, purchase ledgers, and e-way bill data from accounting software.

process.title: ITC Matching & Reconciliation
process.description: Performing automated and manual reconciliation of purchase registers against GSTR-2B portal data.

process.title: Tax Liability Computation
process.description: Calculating net GST payable after eligible ITC set-offs and reverse charge mechanism (RCM) adjustments.

process.title: Filing & Portal Verification
process.description: Filing verified returns on the GSTN portal and archiving return filed acknowledgements.

deliverables.title: Monthly GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B Return Summaries
deliverables.description: Filing confirmation receipts detailing outwards supplies, tax liability, and ITC claimed.

deliverables.title: GSTR-2B vs. Ledger Reconciliation Report
deliverables.description: Monthly report identifying eligible ITC, ineligible ITC, and vendor mismatch items.

deliverables.title: GSTR-9 and GSTR-9C Annual Compliance Documents
deliverables.description: Reconciliation statement comparing audited financial statements with portal filings.

deliverables.title: Export Refund Sanction Orders & Documentation
deliverables.description: Filed RFD-01 applications and tracking receipts for tax refunds.

faqs.question: What is the consequence of claiming ITC not reflected in GSTR-2B?
faqs.answer: Under Rule 36(4) of the CGST Rules, a taxpayer cannot claim ITC unless the supplier has uploaded the invoice in their GSTR-1 and it reflects in the recipient's GSTR-2B. Unmatched ITC claims lead to demand notices with interest under Section 50.

faqs.question: Who is required to generate E-invoices under GST?
faqs.answer: Currently, taxpayers whose aggregate turnover in any preceding financial year from 2017-18 onwards exceeds ₹5 Crores are required to generate Invoice Reference Numbers (IRN) for B2B transactions and exports.

faqs.question: How are GST departmental audit notices addressed?
faqs.answer: Upon receipt of Form GST ADT-01, we assist in preparing reconciliation workpapers, verifying stock registers, confirming outward tax payments, and representing the client before audit authorities.

keyHighlights: Structured monthly vendor ITC reconciliation methodology
Specialized knowledge in export LUT procedures and GST refunds
Proven framework for GSTR-9/9C annual compliance
Strict conformity with GST circulars and CBIC notifications
